strengths: Novel claim-level memory schema with explicit validity intervals and truth-maintenance edges
Session-batched writer reduces token cost 63 % vs A-Mem while adding semantics
First system to couple TMS-style contradict/supersede links with live LLM linking
Clean abstention policy: 80 % explicit IDK on abstention set vs 0 % for Flat RAG
Strict paired dominance on single-session-user (21/21 correct, +2 over Flat)
Open-source release of prompts, code, splits, and per-question outputs
weaknesses: Overall accuracy statistically ties Flat RAG (61.3 % vs 68.0 %, p=0.21) yet claims superiority
Small-slice McNemar tests underpowered (n=9 ABS, n=21 SSU); large effects still non-significant
Router compositions lose TTMG’s ABS advantage (ABS accuracy drops to Flat’s 44 %)
Regressions on SSA/SSP/MS slices (-6.7 pp overall) not fully offset by routers
Intent classifier recall only 42.5 % on multi-session, limiting router ceiling
Full 500-q replication promised but not delivered; only 150-q pilot reported
required_revisions: Provide N=500 LongMemEval-S results or acknowledge as limitation, not future work
Report exact two-sided 95 % CIs for McNemar p-values on n=9 and n=21 slices
Clarify that router compositions do NOT preserve TTMG abstention behaviour on ABS questions
Include power analysis showing minimum detectable effect sizes for n=150 stratified slice
Release the 30-q abstention per-question outputs to verify 76.7 % tied accuracy claim
Discuss why overall accuracy drop is acceptable despite ‘truth maintenance’ headline

strengths: Novel claim-level schema with explicit validity intervals and truth-maintenance edges
Rigorous ablation showing the LLM linker is causally necessary (-2.6 pp overall, -22 pp abstention)
Full reproducibility package: code, seeds, prompts, per-question outputs, McNemar & bootstrap scripts
Efficiency win: 63 % fewer tokens than A-Mem with lower latency
Statistically significant large-effect wins on abstention (h=1.0) and single-session-user (h=0.63)
weaknesses: Only 150-question stratified subset for primary claims; 500-question replication missing
McNemar p-values on key slices (ABS n=9, SSU n=21) are under-powered (p≥0.06) yet interpreted as conclusive
No error bars or significance tests on ablation drops (Table 5)
Router results inherit Flat-RAG accuracy without preserving TTMG abstention benefit on ABS slice
Reader model and embedding frozen; no sensitivity analysis to encoder or reader scale
required_revisions: Run and report the full 500-question LongMemEval-S for TTMG with CIs and McNemar vs Flat
Add paired CIs and two-sided exact McNemar p-values for every ablation delta in Table 5
Provide per-question discordant counts for the --contradict ablation to enable significance testing
Include a variance analysis: bootstrap or paired CIs on token counts and latency per question
Discuss statistical power: state upfront that n=9/21 slices are trend-level only and require larger N

strengths: Claim-level truth-maintenance schema is novel for conversational memory
Session-batched writer cuts token cost 63% vs A-Mem
Explicit abstention policy works on 80% of abstention probes
Router composition recovers Flat-RAG overall accuracy while keeping TTMG benefits
Full source, prompts, seeds, and per-question outputs released
weaknesses: Overall accuracy statistically ties Flat RAG (61.3% vs 68.0%, p=0.21)
LLM linker hyper-parameters (τ_hard=0.7, bypass=0.85) not sensitivity-checked
Intent classifier recall only 42.5% on multi-session slice, limiting router gain
Regressions on SSA/SSP/MS slices traced to over-abstention from narrow claim context
Evaluation still on 150-question subset; 500-question run promised for camera-ready
required_revisions: Run full N=500 LongMemEval-S and report confidence intervals
Ablation table needs standard errors or bootstrap CIs to assert -2.6 pp drop is real
Provide latency CDF or P99, not only mean, to show tail behaviour
Document throughput (queries/s) under concurrent load, not single-thread latency
Sensitivity sweep on linker thresholds (τ_sim, τ_bypass, τ_hard) and k_keep

strengths: Well-motivated problem: truth maintenance for long-horizon agent memory is timely and under-studied
Clean conceptual design: claim schema with validity intervals, contradict/supersede edges, and maximum-consistent subgraph retrieval form a coherent system
Commendably honest evaluation: transparent about regressions, underpowered slices, and failure modes (F1-F3 taxonomy)
Ablation cleanly isolates the LLM linker's causal role with drops on every measured slice
Efficiency gains over A-Mem are meaningful (-63.5% tokens) and well-documented
Oracle upper bound (82.0%) usefully quantifies complementarity between claim-level and raw-text retrieval
weaknesses: Overall accuracy (61.3%) is numerically lower than Flat RAG (68.0%); the paper frames parity via an underpowered McNemar test rather than acknowledging regression
Key wins are on small slices: abstention n=9 (p=0.125 two-sided, not significant), SSU n=21 (paired 2-0, p=0.50, not significant)
Full abstention set (n=30) shows TTMG and Flat RAG tied at 76.7% — the abstention accuracy advantage disappears at scale; only behavioral difference (explicit abstention) remains
Deployable routers (67.3%, 66.0%) do not improve over Flat RAG (68.0%) even numerically, undermining the composition argument
Evaluation on only 150/500 questions; full N=500 TTMG run is deferred to 'camera-ready future work', leaving representativeness partially unverified for TTMG
Regressions on SSA, SSP, MS are structural to the claim-as-atom design and are not addressed beyond fallback routing
required_revisions: Run and report TTMG on the full 500-question LongMemEval-S to verify subset representativeness for TTMG (currently only verified for Flat RAG)
Either improve the deployable router to demonstrably exceed Flat RAG overall, or reframe claims to accurately reflect that composition matches but does not beat the baseline
Address the full abstention set result honestly in the abstract and introduction: TTMG and Flat RAG are tied on outcome accuracy at n=30; the advantage is behavioral only
Report effect sizes alongside all p-values consistently; currently some slices report only p-values without acknowledging underpowered designs
Investigate and report whether increasing k_keep or multi-claim reader prompts close the MS/SSA regression gap, even in preliminary form

strengths: Honest reporting of non-significant p-values and effect sizes alongside McNemar tests
Clear failure-mode taxonomy (F1-F3) explaining regressions on SSA/SSP/MS slices
Well-motivated conceptual contribution: truth maintenance lens for agent memory
Good reproducibility commitment: code, prompts, per-question outputs, fixed seed
Ablation cleanly isolates the LLM linker's causal role with per-slice drops
Oracle upper bound analysis quantifies complementarity of claim-level vs raw-text retrieval
weaknesses: TTMG is directionally WORSE overall than Flat RAG (61.3% vs 68.0%, p=0.21) — headline claims rest on non-significant subset wins
Key slice wins are underpowered: SSU n=21 paired 2-0 gives p=0.50; ABS n=9 gives p=0.125; full ABS n=30 shows p=1.00 with identical accuracy
No compute-matched comparison: TTMG uses 12.4x more tokens than Flat RAG (13603 vs 1100), yet still loses overall
A-Mem baseline is an unvalidated reimplementation with no comparison to original paper's published numbers
Only one ablation (remove linker); missing ablations for claim schema alone, subject-predicate gate, hybrid fallback, k values, thresholds
Only evaluated on one benchmark (LongMemEval-S); no LoCoMo or MemoryAgentBench despite citing them as motivation
TTMG not evaluated on full N=500; representativeness only verified for Flat RAG
Explicit abstention behavioral difference does not translate to accuracy gain on full abstention set (both 76.7%)
Deployable routers (67.3%, 66.0%) still don't beat Flat RAG (68.0%) overall
required_revisions: Run TTMG on the full N=500 LongMemEval-S to verify subset representativeness and increase statistical power on key slices
Add compute-matched baselines: either give Flat RAG equivalent token budget (e.g., more retrieval, reranking) or report TTMG accuracy under Flat RAG's token budget
Validate A-Mem reimplementation against original paper's published numbers on overlapping benchmarks

Add ablations isolating: (a) claim schema without validity intervals, (b) subject-predicate gate vs LLM-only linking, (c) hybrid raw-turn fallback removal, (d) sensitivity to k_keep and similarity thresholds
Evaluate on at least one additional benchmark (LoCoMo or MemoryAgentBench) to demonstrate generality
Tone down headline claims that rest on non-significant paired comparisons; explicitly flag SSU and ABS wins as trend-level with medium effect sizes but insufficient power
Report per-question latency breakdown including writer/linker amortization to justify the efficiency comparison framing

strengths: Well-motivated problem: truth maintenance in agent memory is genuinely under-studied and important
Clean formalization of claim schema with validity intervals, contradict/supersede edges, and maximum-consistent-subgraph retrieval
Honest and thorough reporting: regressions are explicitly catalogued with a clear failure-mode taxonomy (F1-F3)
Strong statistical methodology: paired McNemar tests, Cohen's h effect sizes, bootstrap CIs, per-question dominance analysis
Ablation cleanly isolates the LLM linker as the causal driver of gains across all slices
Session-batched writer and subject/predicate gate are practical systems optimizations that reduce cost vs A-Mem
Router composition strategy is a realistic deployment path that recovers overall parity
weaknesses: Overall accuracy is directionally worse than Flat RAG (61.3% vs 68.0%) — hard to justify deploying a more complex system that regresses on the bottom line
Token cost is 12.4x higher than Flat RAG (13603 vs 1100 tokens/q); cost savings are only vs A-Mem, not vs the practical baseline
Key abstention benefit is behavioral (explicit 'I don't know') not accuracy-improving: both systems tie at 76.7% on the full n=30 abstention set
Router compositions do NOT preserve the abstention benefit (ABS accuracy equals Flat's by construction) — the headline advantage is lost in the deployable system
Evaluation on only 150/500 questions; full-scale run deferred to camera-ready is a significant gap for a systems paper
Ingestion latency of 80.3s/question is impractical for real-time deployment even if amortized across sessions
Intent classifier is weak (64.7% overall, 42.5% MS recall), leaving a 15pp gap to oracle — the router is fragile
Regresses on 3 of 6 slices (SSA -35pp, SSP -33pp, MS -7.5pp) due to structural limitations of claim-as-atom abstraction
required_revisions: Run and report full N=500 LongMemEval-S evaluation; a systems paper cannot defer the primary scale verification
Report per-component latency breakdown (writer vs linker vs parser vs embedding) to identify bottlenecks and justify the 80.3s ingestion cost
Compare token cost directly against Flat RAG, not just A-Mem — the 12x cost increase over the practical baseline must be addressed
Design a router that preserves abstention benefit (e.g., union of TTMG abstain-flag and intent classifier) rather than noting its absence as future work
Report memory/storage overhead of the claim graph vs flat index (number of claims, edges, index size) — critical for deployment sizing
Address the ingestion latency: can the writer/linker be parallelized or further gated? 80s/q is not deployable
Provide a cost-accuracy Pareto analysis across all three systems to make the tradeoff explicit
